annotations.label: Partial function

anchor.snippet: a partial function f from a set X to a set Y is a function from a subset S of X

annotations.note: `PFun α β` is defined as `α → Part β`, directly modeling a partial function from `α` to `β`.

annotations.label: Domain of definition / natural domain

anchor.snippet: is called the domain of definition or natural domain of f

annotations.note: `PFun.Dom f` is the set `{a | (f a).Dom}` of inputs on which `f` is defined.

annotations.label: Total function

anchor.snippet: then [MATH] is said to be a total function

annotations.note: Total functions are the ordinary `α → β`; `PFun.lift` coerces them to `PFun` and `PFun.dom_coe` shows the domain is `Set.univ`, but no standalone `Total` predicate is defined on `PFun`.

annotations.label: Partial function as univalent relation

anchor.snippet: a partial function is a binary relation over two sets that associates to every element of the first set at most one element of the second set

annotations.note: `PFun.graph'` turns a partial function into a `Set`-valued relation, but the equivalence with univalent relations is not packaged as a theorem.

annotations.label: General recursive function

anchor.snippet: a general recursive function is a partial function from the integers to the integers

annotations.note: `Nat.Partrec` is the inductive predicate on `ℕ →. ℕ` capturing partial recursive functions.

annotations.label: Square root restricted to integers

anchor.snippet: is the square root function restricted to the integers

annotations.note: No explicit partial function for integer square root restricted to perfect squares appears; `Nat.sqrt` is total.

annotations.label: Square root as partial function on reals

anchor.snippet: because negative real numbers do not have real square roots, the operation can be viewed as a partial function

annotations.note: `Real.sqrt` is total in Mathlib (returns 0 on negatives) rather than encoded as a `PFun` on ℝ.

annotations.label: Domain of definition (subset S)

anchor.snippet: The domain of definition of a partial function is the subset S of X on which the partial function is defined

annotations.note: Same `PFun.Dom` declaration: a `Set α` consisting of the inputs on which `f` is defined.

annotations.label: Total partial function

anchor.snippet: In case the domain of definition S is equal to the whole set X , the partial function is said to be total

annotations.note: `PFun.dom_coe` shows lifted total functions have `Dom = Set.univ`, but no standalone `PFun.Total` predicate is defined.

annotations.label: Injective/surjective/bijective partial function

anchor.snippet: A partial function is said to be injective , surjective , or bijective when the function given by the restriction of the partial function to its domain of definition is injective, surjective, or bijective, respectively

annotations.note: No `PFun.Injective`/`PFun.Surjective`/`PFun.Bijective` predicate exists; one can express these via `PFun.asSubtype` and `Function.Injective`, but the named predicates are absent.

annotations.label: Partial bijection

anchor.snippet: the term partial bijection denotes a partial function which is injective

annotations.note: `PEquiv α β` represents a bijection between subsets of `α` and `β` via `Option`-valued partial functions, capturing the partial-bijection notion.

annotations.label: Inversion of injective partial functions

anchor.snippet: An injective partial function may be inverted to an injective partial function

annotations.note: `PEquiv.symm` inverts a partial bijection to another partial bijection, but no general inversion theorem for an injective `PFun` is stated.

annotations.label: Injection invertible to bijective partial function

anchor.snippet: a function which is injective may be inverted to a bijective partial function

annotations.note: Mathlib does not package a theorem inverting an injective total `α → β` to a bijective `PFun β α`; one can build this from `Function.invFun` plus `PFun`/`PEquiv`.

annotations.label: Partial transformation

anchor.snippet: A partial transformation is a function

annotations.note: A partial transformation `α →. α` is a special case of `PFun α α`, but no dedicated `PartialTransformation` name exists.

annotations.label: Set of partial functions

anchor.snippet: denote the set of all partial functions

annotations.note: The type `PFun α β` itself plays the role of the set of all partial functions from `α` to `β`.

annotations.label: Cardinality of partial function set

anchor.snippet: In finite case, its cardinality is

annotations.note: No cardinality formula `(|Y|+1)^|X|` for `PFun X Y` in the finite case is stated in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Diagram example

anchor.snippet: The first diagram at the top of the article represents a partial function that is not a function

annotations.note: Pictorial example with no formal counterpart.

annotations.label: Natural logarithm

anchor.snippet: The natural logarithm function mapping the real numbers to themselves is a partial function

annotations.note: `Real.log` is defined as a total function (zero on non-positives) rather than as a `PFun`.

annotations.label: Subtraction of natural numbers

anchor.snippet: Subtraction of natural numbers

annotations.note: `Nat.sub` is a total truncated subtraction in core/Mathlib, not modeled as a `PFun`.

annotations.label: Bottom element semantics

anchor.snippet: In denotational semantics a partial function is considered as returning the bottom element when it is undefined

annotations.note: `Part.none` plays the role of the bottom/undefined value for `Part`-valued partial functions, but no explicit denotational ⊥-semantics framework is developed.

annotations.label: Partial function as subroutine

anchor.snippet: In computer science a partial function corresponds to a subroutine that raises an exception or loops forever

annotations.note: Informal CS analogy, not a Mathlib statement.

annotations.label: Composition is total iff hom has one element

anchor.snippet: the composition operation [MATH] is a total function if and only if

annotations.note: This characterization of when composition is total in the partial-function category is not stated in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Equivalence with pointed sets

anchor.snippet: The category of sets and partial functions is equivalent to but not isomorphic with the category of pointed sets

annotations.note: `partialFunEquivPointed : PartialFun ≌ Pointed` gives the categorical equivalence between sets-with-partial-functions and pointed sets.

annotations.label: Self-duality of partial bijections category

anchor.snippet: The category of sets and partial bijections is equivalent to its dual

annotations.note: Neither the category of sets-with-partial-bijections nor its self-duality is formalized.

annotations.label: Partial algebra

anchor.snippet: Partial algebra generalizes the notion of universal algebra to partial operations

annotations.note: No `PartialAlgebra` or analogous notion was found in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Field as partial algebra

anchor.snippet: An example would be a field , in which the multiplicative inversion is the only proper partial operation

annotations.note: Mathlib's `Field` uses total inversion with `0⁻¹ = 0`, not a partial operation; no partial-algebra view of fields is recorded.

annotations.label: Semigroup of partial transformations

anchor.snippet: forms a regular semigroup called the semigroup of all partial transformations

annotations.note: No semigroup-of-partial-transformations construction is present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Symmetric inverse semigroup

anchor.snippet: The set of all partial bijections on [MATH] forms the symmetric inverse semigroup

annotations.note: Mathlib does not formalize the symmetric inverse semigroup on a set.

annotations.label: Charts in atlases as partial functions

anchor.section: Charts and atlases for manifolds and fiber bundles
anchor.snippet: Charts in the atlases which specify the structure of manifolds and fiber bundles are partial functions

annotations.note: `ChartedSpace H M` has an atlas of `OpenPartialHomeomorph`s, embodying charts-as-partial-functions but via partial homeomorphisms rather than via `PFun`.

annotations.label: Transition map

anchor.section: Charts and atlases for manifolds and fiber bundles
anchor.snippet: the most important construction is the transition map , which is the composite of one chart with the inverse of another

annotations.note: Transition maps between charts are realized as `OpenPartialHomeomorph.trans`, composing one chart with the `symm` of another over the maximal domain where it is defined.
